entities.name: Bureau of Indian Affairs (BIA)
entities.context: Federal agency responsible for Indian affairs, including lease administration and land management on Crow Indian Reservation

entities.name: BIA Billings Area Office (BAO)
entities.context: Regional BIA office responsible for lease administration on Crow Indian Reservation

entities.name: Office of Inspector General
entities.context: United States Department of the Interior office that conducted audit of Crow Indian Reservation land purchases

entities.name: United States Department of the Interior
entities.context: Federal department overseeing BIA and Indian Affairs

entities.name: Office of the Solicitor
entities.context: Legal office within Department of Interior handling Indian Affairs legal matters

entities.name: Division of Indian Affairs
entities.context: Division within Office of the Solicitor handling Indian Affairs

entities.name: Department of Justice
entities.context: Federal department requested to litigate Section 2 violations

entities.name: Native American Rights Fund (NARF)
entities.context: Organization that identified 56 potential Section 2 violators owning about 285,000 acres; worked with Crow Tribe from 1974

entities.name: Crow Tribe of Montana
entities.context: Plaintiff in Civil Action No. 87-2155; subject of land protection under Crow Allotment Act of 1920

entities.name: Crow Tribal Council
entities.context: Governing body of Crow Tribe that passed numerous resolutions regarding Section 2 enforcement

entities.name: Crow Indian Reservation
entities.context: Reservation in Montana subject to land acquisition restrictions under Section 2 of Crow Allotment Act of 1920

entities.name: Crow Allotment Act of 1920
entities.context: 41 Stat. 751; contains Section 2 restricting land acquisition on Crow Indian Reservation; passed June 4, 1920

entities.name: Section 2 of Crow Allotment Act
entities.context: Provision restricting land acquisition to 1,280 acres agricultural or 1,920 acres grazing within Crow Indian Reservation

entities.name: Fort Laramie Treaty of 1868
entities.context: Treaty concluded May 7, 1868, ratified July 25, 1868, proclaimed August 12, 1868; basis for Crow Tribe land claims

entities.name: Title 25 Code of Federal Regulations (25 CFR)
entities.context: Federal regulations where Section 2 restrictions were never published

entities.name: 42 U.S.C. 1983
entities.context: Federal civil rights statute under which Crow Tribe sought to amend complaint

entities.name: Fifth Amendment
entities.context: Due process and just compensation clauses cited regarding constitutionality of ratifying Section 2 violations

entities.name: Dillon Case
entities.context: Test case involving fee-patented land; Justice Department declined to litigate; decision used by Solicitor's Office as justification for not pursuing Section 2

entities.name: Civil Action No. 87-2155
entities.context: Crow Tribe of Montana v. United States of America et al., filed in United States District Court for District of Columbia

entities.name: Big Horn River Case
entities.context: 1981 case referenced in context of land loss and jurisdiction/sovereignty

entities.name: Heckion vs U.S. 224 U.S. 413
entities.context: Cited as precedent for government restoring illegally sold lands, notably for Cherokees

entities.name: 61 Montana 322, White Bear vs Barth
entities.context: Montana case referenced regarding large grazing tract disputes in 1915-1916

entities.name: Homer Scott
entities.context: Largest non-Indian owner of Crow reservation lands, reputed to own 66,395 acres; subject of proposed test case; barred from leasing or buying Crow lands by tribal resolution

events.description: Congress passed the Crow Allotment Act (41 Stat. 751) including Section 2 restricting land acquisition on Crow Indian Reservation
Fort Laramie Treaty concluded between Crow Tribe and United States; Crow Tribe gave up more than 38.5 million acres of what are today Wyoming and Montana
